expected_registry.P2: Mexican Mint (Black Pot | White Rabbit anchor)

observed_plants.details: Not observed in any image where P2_Black_Pot is present.
Appears from T-3 onwards in the P2_Black_Pot. Morphology does not match Mexican Mint.
Small green leaf/fragment observed in Unregistered_White_Cup from T-1 onwards.

observed_anchors.details: The white rabbit scale anchor was not observed in any image. Registry states P3, but expected biome registry states P2. Neither location shows the rabbit.

inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss (Expected plant not present in the black pot. Instead, bare soil or a different plant is observed).
inventory_reconciliation.White_Rabbit_Anchor: Systemic Loss (Expected scale anchor not visible in any image).
inventory_reconciliation.Unregistered_Yellow_Pot: Systemic Loss (Present in EARLIEST and T-5, then removed from frame).
inventory_reconciliation.New_Plant_in_P2_Black_Pot: New Introduction (A new plant, not Mexican Mint, appears in the black pot from T-3 onwards).
inventory_reconciliation.Eggshell_Fragments_in_P2_Black_Pot: New Introduction/Intervention (Present in the black pot from EARLIEST onwards).
inventory_reconciliation.Electronic_Sensor_and_Wires: New Introduction/Intervention (Present on desk near P2_Black_Pot from T-3 onwards).
inventory_reconciliation.Blue_Book_and_Pen: New Introduction/Intervention (Present on desk near P2_Black_Pot from T-3 onwards).

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: Black pot contains dark soil with several small, irregular white fragments (likely eggshells). No plant material visible.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: No plant to assess. Soil appears moist.

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: Consistent with EARLIEST. Black pot contains dark soil with white fragments. No plant material visible.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: No plant to assess. Soil appears moist.

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: Image is completely black. No visual information available.
A small, young plant with approximately 4-5 rounded, dark green leaves is present in the black pot. Leaves appear turgid. White fragments are still visible in the soil.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: Plant appears healthy, good turgor and color.

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: The plant appears consistent with T-3, possibly slightly larger or more clearly defined due to improved lighting. Leaves are rounded, dark green, and turgid. White fragments remain.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: Plant appears healthy, good turgor and color.

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: The plant's size and health appear consistent with T-2. Leaves are dark green and turgid. White fragments are still present.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: Plant appears healthy, good turgor and color.

P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.description: The plant is visible, but the image is significantly darker, making detailed assessment difficult. Leaves appear dark green, no obvious wilting or discoloration discernible.
P2_Black_Pot_Occupant.health_inference: Appears generally healthy, but precise assessment is hindered by low light. No immediate signs of distress.

Unregistered_White_Cup_Occupant.description: White cup not present in the frame.
A small green leaf or plant fragment is visible submerged or floating in water within the newly introduced white cup.
Unregistered_White_Cup_Occupant.health_inference: Fragment appears green and viable, possibly a cutting.

Unregistered_White_Cup_Occupant.description: The green fragment is still visible in the water, though obscured by the overall image darkness.
Unregistered_White_Cup_Occupant.health_inference: Appears generally viable, but precise assessment is hindered by low light.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Generally dark and appears moist in all visible pots throughout the sequence. No signs of cracking or excessive dryness.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in any pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface_debris: Dark desk surface. Wires, an electronic sensor board, a blue book, and a pen are present from T-3 onwards. White fragments (eggshells) are consistently present in the black pot's soil.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-5: Minimal change; slight shift in camera angle or minor lighting variation. Composition remains two pots (yellow/gold and black) with bare soil and eggshells in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-4: Complete loss of visual data; image is entirely black.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Major compositional shift: The yellow/gold pot is removed. A new plant is introduced into the black pot. Electronic sensor, wires, a blue book, and a pen are introduced onto the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Improved overall lighting and clarity, making the plant and soil in the black pot more discernible. Plant appears consistent or slightly grown.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Introduction of a new white cup containing water and a small green plant fragment to the right side of the frame.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Significant reduction in overall image lighting, making detailed observation challenging.

visual_health_inference.P2_Black_Pot_Plant: The introduced plant in the black pot (from T-3 to T-1) exhibits characteristics of good health: dark green, turgid, rounded leaves, with no visible signs of wilting, chlorosis, or necrosis. In the CURRENT, darker image, no immediate distress signals are apparent, suggesting continued health, though precise assessment is limited.
visual_health_inference.Unregistered_White_Cup_Plant_Fragment: The green fragment in the white cup (T-1 to CURRENT) appears viable, suggesting it is alive, possibly a cutting being propagated.

systemic_losses: Mexican Mint (P2) - Expected plant not found.
White Rabbit anchor - Expected scale anchor not found.
Unregistered Yellow/Gold Pot - Removed from scene after T-5.
new_introductions_interventions: Electronic sensor, wires, blue book, and pen on desk (T-3 onwards).
Unregistered White Cup with water and plant fragment (T-1 onwards).
monitoring_anomalies: Image [T-4] is completely black, resulting in a total loss of visual data for that timestamp.
Image [CURRENT] exhibits significantly reduced lighting, hindering detailed visual assessment.

vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical observation period reveals a dynamic environment. Initially, the scene comprised a yellow/gold pot and a black pot, both containing soil. The black pot also contained eggshell fragments, but the expected Mexican Mint and white rabbit anchor were absent, indicating a systemic loss. A critical data gap occurred on T-4 with a completely black image. Following this, the scene underwent a significant transformation by T-3: the yellow/gold pot was removed, and a new, healthy-looking plant with rounded, dark green leaves was introduced into the black pot. Electronic monitoring equipment, a book, and a pen also appeared on the desk. This new plant maintained good turgor and color through T-1. On T-1, a new white cup containing water and a small green plant fragment was introduced. The final image (CURRENT) is notably darker, making precise assessment challenging, but the plants appear to maintain their general form without obvious signs of decline. The overall health of the introduced plant in the black pot appears good, and the fragment in the water also seems viable, possibly as a cutting.
vision_report.confidence: High. Visual evidence is clear for compositional changes, plant presence, and general health inferences, with acknowledged limitations due to the black image (T-4) and the low-light current image.
